q: The core of a transformer is built up of thin iron sheets called lamination.
The current delivered by a battery is a direct current.
A changing magnetic field could produce a current.
An AC generator is a device for generating direct current.
The current flowing in power lines is direct current.
Lenz's law is based on the law of conservation of energy.
In a DC, the electrons flow steadily in the same direction all the time.
The voltage used in Myanmar is between 220 V and 240 V.
A galvanometer is used to measure very small current.
The alternating current is universally used for household electricity.
The magnetic flux through a surface area is Φ = BA cos θ.
The SI unit of magnetic flux is the Tesla.
One Weber is equal to one Tesla-metre squared.
If the magnetic field is parallel to the surface, the magnetic flux is maximum.
The induced emf is proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux.
A transformer can change the voltage of a direct current.
The transformer coil connected to an electrical device is called the secondary coil.
In a step-up transformer, the number of turns in the secondary coil is less than the primary.
Eddy currents cause energy loss in the form of heat in the transformer core.
The self-inductance of a coil depends on the shape and size of the coil.
The SI unit of self-inductance is the Henry.
An electric motor converts mechanical energy into electrical energy.
The frequency of alternating current in Myanmar is 50 Hz.
Mutual induction is the production of induced emf in one coil due to current change in another.
The back emf in a motor is zero when the motor is at rest.
Magnetic flux is a vector quantity.
In an ideal transformer, the efficiency is 100%.
The lamination of the core increases the effect of eddy currents.
The current in a cell phone is an alternating current.
A step-down transformer decreases the voltage and increases the current.
The primary coil of a transformer is connected to an alternating voltage source.
Self-induction is sometimes called the 'inertia of electricity'.
The direction of the induced current is such as to oppose the change that produces it.
The induced emf depends on the resistance of the coil.
A rotating coil in a magnetic field produces a sinusoidal alternating emf.
Transformers are used to provide different voltage requirements from the mains supply.
A DC generator uses slip rings to deliver current.
The ignition system of motor cars uses an induction coil.
The unit Henry is equivalent to Weber per Ampere.
Soft iron is used for the core of a transformer because it is easily magnetized.
If the magnetic flux through a coil is constant, an emf is induced.
The output power of a transformer can never exceed the input power.
The alternating current changes its direction periodically.
The induced current in a circuit is larger if the circuit resistance is higher.
Secondary wavelets travel at the same speed as the primary wave.
The transformer ratio is given by Vs/Vp = Ns/Np.
A flashlight works on alternating current.
Faraday discovered that a moving magnet can induce a current in a coil.
The back emf opposes the applied voltage in an electric motor.
The self-induced emf is also known as back emf.
